title: World-Record Perovskite Module
summary: Led all modulization loss mechanisms to a world-record 24% efficiency module, jointly developed with NLR (formerly NREL).

par.problem: Modulizing perovskite solar cells introduced loss mechanisms that capped both efficiency and degradation lifespan.
par.action: As lead of module development, owned every loss mechanism related to cell modulization and drove joint development with NLR (formerly NREL).
par.result: Achieved a world-record 24.0% efficiency module, a 35% relative gain in initial module efficiency, and a 35× improvement in degradation lifespan. Work published in Science (2025).

title: Laser Scribing Automation & Machine Vision
summary: Automated laser scribing with machine-vision alignment; 500% throughput and repeatability from 60μm to 20μm.

par.problem: Manual laser scribing was slow, engineer-dependent, and positionally inconsistent across substrates.
par.action: Automated the process with a Sherlock machine-vision system to detect glass-edge position and skew, and built a parameterized, recipe-based UI that adapts to evolving production needs, transitioning operation from engineer to technician level.
par.result: Increased throughput 500% and yield 5%, cut takt time 33%, and improved 6σ positional repeatability from 60μm to 20μm.

title: Company-Wide Defect Tracking System
summary: Identified two defect types on 50% of modules driving 20× the degradation impact of all others combined.

par.problem: Degradation drivers were not systematically tracked, so process improvements were not data-driven.
par.action: Spearheaded a defect tracking initiative, built a Pareto linking frequency and severity to degradation, and authored the functional specification for the software team.
par.result: Identified two defect types present on 50% of modules causing 20× the degradation impact of all other categories combined, enabling company-wide rollout and large-dataset-driven process improvements.

title: NIR Characterization Jig
summary: Scoped, designed, and delivered a custom near-infrared characterization fixture, requirements to assembled tool.

par.problem: Characterization work needed a repeatable NIR fixture with no off-the-shelf option available.
par.action: Gathered requirements with stakeholders, pitched two concepts, modeled the selected design in SolidWorks, then 3D printed and assembled it.
par.result: Jig assembled and installed in the lab, enabling repeatable NIR characterization of substrates.

title: SS2 RobotUnit Frame
summary: Designed, fabricated, and installed a structural frame for the SS2 outdoor test system.

par.problem: The SS2 test station needed a robot-unit frame consistent with the existing SS1 design and lab constraints.
par.action: Measured the SS1 frame, modeled SS2 in SolidWorks, cut stock, and assembled the structure.
par.result: Frame fabricated and installed, enabling outdoor degradation testing of production modules.

title: DMC Tray Scanner
summary: Designed and built a linear DMC scanning system that captures each substrate's tray position in a single pass, cutting scan time ~90%.

par.problem: Single-substrate scanning was slow and did not link tray position to downstream performance data.
par.action: Designed and built a linear Data Matrix Code scanning system that captures each substrate's tray position in a single scanner pass across 3 manufacturing processes.
par.result: Reduced substrate scan time by ~90% and enabled tray-position tracking that links substrate location during a process to its performance data.

title: Large-Module Photobooth
summary: Converted a photoluminescence box into a multi-mode photobooth for visual characterization of 400mm substrates.

par.problem: No production-scale visual characterization tool existed for 400mm substrates.
par.action: Converted a large-area photoluminescence box into a multi-mode photobooth, engineering a 3D-printed lighting mount to block light leakage and diffuse output, and installed dual-illumination with external mode-switching.
par.result: Delivered an operator-ready characterization tool with validated backlight and toplight imaging modes at production scale.

title: SDC DMC Scanning System
summary: Designed and installed two DMC scanning stations in coaters for automated traceability of coating events.

par.problem: Coating events were not automatically linked to substrate identity, limiting traceability.
par.action: Designed, installed, and tuned two DMC scanning stations in coaters, iterating hardware based on production team feedback for glass clearance, particle reduction, and scan-success indication.
par.result: Enabled automated traceability of coating events with an operator-ready system shaped by production feedback.

title: F-35 Ground Equipment Cost-Benefit Analysis
summary: Lead author of an analysis adopted by Luke AFB, saving $20,700 (73%) annually.

par.problem: Diverted F-35 aircraft at Gila Bend Auxiliary Airfield required aerospace ground equipment transported from Luke AFB at significant recurring cost.
par.action: Led a cost-benefit analysis of stationing options and authored the final report for the sponsor and program operator.
par.result: Recommended strategy saved $20,700 (73%) annually with minimal workload increase, was implemented at Luke AFB, and was slated for nationwide rollout.

title: Non-Invasive Intracranial Pressure Prototype
summary: Built a prototype testbed evaluating non-invasive intracranial pressure measurement via ultrasound.

par.problem: Measuring intracranial pressure in patients with acute neurologic injury traditionally requires invasive methods.
par.action: Developed a user-friendly SolidWorks prototype to collect patient data and ran end-user interviews against existing solutions.
par.result: Enabled clinical evaluation of a non-invasive ultrasound-based ICP measurement method.

title: Production Line Enclosure Systems
summary: Designed bespoke soundproofing, heat-recovery, and climate enclosure systems for cardboard production lines.

par.problem: Corrugated cardboard production lines needed bespoke enclosure systems for sound, heat recovery, and climate control.
par.action: Designed and modeled the dry-end enclosure in SolidWorks and improved the company's pack-and-go subassembly workflow.
par.result: Produced production-ready master assemblies of 1,000+ components and streamlined the company's subassembly packaging workflow.

title: Adaptive Bike Shift & Brake Mechanism
video: assets/Adaptive Road Bike Shifter and Braking Mechanism Demonstration Video.mp4
summary: Adaptive shifters that move actuation from wrist to shoulder for a quadriplegic cyclist.

par.problem: Our need-knower with incomplete quadriplegia could not use his handcycle, as braking and shifting required a wrist motion he was weak in.
par.action: Designed, manufactured, and tested a mechanism that shifts actuation from the hand to along the forearm, enabling a shoulder motion instead.
par.result: Won 'Most Likely to be Commercialized' at the Jacobs Showcase.

title: Automated Cling Wrap Dispenser
video: assets/Automated Cling Wrap Dispenser Demonstration Video.mp4
summary: Built an automated dispenser; managed all electronics and wrote PI controllers in C++ on an ESP32.

par.problem: Manual cling-wrap dispensing is slow and awkward in food prep and storage.
par.action: Built an automated dispenser, managing motors, drivers, and analog/digital sensors and writing PI controllers for the arm and slicer in C++.
par.result: Produced a fully functional automated dispenser driven by an ESP32 microcontroller with closed-loop motor control.

title: Climate Change & Contamination Report
summary: Co-authored a report for Greenaction for Health and Environmental Justice detailing how climate change will exacerbate historical contamination in the San Francisco Bay Area.

par.problem: Climate change processes such as sea-level rise and groundwater rise threaten to reactivate historically contaminated sites across the San Francisco Bay Area, but the intersection of these risks was not well documented for advocacy.
par.action: As a research intern with Greenaction, co-authored a four-case-study report; primary author of the Bayview Hunters Point case study covering the site's radiological and industrial contamination history, health impacts, cleanup fraud, and ongoing environmental injustice.
par.result: Report adopted by Greenaction to advocate for government and press attention to contamination cleanup before climate change worsens its effects on vulnerable communities.

title: Model Wind Turbine
summary: Designed a model turbine and the AC-DC circuitry to deliver constant regulated power.

par.problem: The brief required a constant 10 W at 5.5 V across wind speeds of 10–20 m/s.
par.action: Contributed to the overall design and built the circuitry converting generator AC to regulated DC.
par.result: Achieved constant 10 W regulated output at 5.5 V across the full 10–20 m/s wind speed range.

title: NASA Micro-g NExT RABeT System

metric.label: tested at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Lab

summary: Designed a one-handed EVA tool attachment for astronaut utility belts, tested by NASA at the NBL.

par.problem: NASA's Micro-g NExT challenge sought a quick-release system letting astronauts attach EVA tools to their utility belt.
par.action: Designed, proposed, and manufactured the Rapid Attachment Belt and Tool system for blind, one-handed operation; served as Editor-in-Chief of the technical report using FEA, FMEA, and trade studies.
par.result: Selected for Phase 2 and tested at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Laboratory, noted for intuitive design and resilience to lunar regolith.

title: Rocket Recovery System
summary: Designed a black-powderless CO2 recovery system and modeled descent dynamics in MATLAB.

par.problem: The team's 9 km rocket needed a recovery system to replace the prior black-powder approach.
par.action: Designed a motor-driven lead-screw mechanism puncturing a CO2 canister to deploy the parachutes, and modeled descent characteristics in MATLAB to size the drogue and main chutes.
par.result: Recovery system validated with correctly sized drogue and main chutes for the rocket's descent profile.

title: Flight Emissions Automation
summary: Automated a hundreds-of-hours emissions task; cut runtime from 15+ minutes to under 10 seconds.

par.problem: Calculating emissions for thousands of study-abroad student flights would have taken hundreds of hours by hand.
par.action: Automated the calculation using airport latitude/longitude lookups, then implemented a binary search to replace a 15-minute-per-iteration linear scan.
par.result: Cut runtime to under 10 seconds, completing in seconds what would have taken hundreds of hours.

title: High-Pressure Water Rocket Launcher
summary: Built an 80 psi water-rocket launcher and flew an onboard camera to estimate a 300 ft apogee.

par.problem: Wanted a high-powered, reusable water-rocket platform.
par.action: Designed and built a launcher pressurized to 80 psi and flew an onboard camera for flight data.
par.result: Estimated a ~300 ft apogee using the onboard footage and a field reference.

title: Burst-Disk Nerf Air Cannon
summary: Designed a burst-disk pneumatic cannon reaching velocities that required reinforcing the projectiles.

par.problem: A physics project called for a high-velocity projectile launcher.
par.action: Built a pressure chamber and barrel separated by a plastic burst disk, pressurizing to a few hundred psi before release.
par.result: Air velocity was high enough that Nerf darts had to be reinforced with tape; metal hex drivers embedded inches into a wooden backstop.

title: Designed & Built Coffee Table
summary: Modeled a coffee table in SolidWorks from spare lumber, then built, stained, and finished it.

par.problem: Wanted a coffee table for my apartment using leftover wood.
par.action: Modeled the design in SolidWorks, then built, stained, and applied a protective finish.
par.result: Used the table throughout the rest of my undergraduate program.
